content.claim: Governance system instability itself generates resource costs distinct from demographic drivers: the system oscillated between decentralization (pre-2013), centralization (2013), and partial re-decentralization (2017+), with each transition creating adjustment costs in staff, administration, and capital planning regardless of closure/retention decisions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Adjustment/transition costs asserted but not quantified
No comparison of total governance transition costs against different policy scenarios
Unclear whether instability is intrinsic to the problem or reflects political choice
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
